description:
You are a compliance analyst who has just approved a patch-level change to an internal
    project located at

        /home/user/project

    The repository already contains two key files that must be updated:

        1. /home/user/project/VERSION
        2. /home/user/project/CHANGELOG.md

    Goal
    ----
    1. Bump the present semantic version from 1.4.2 to 1.4.3 (PATCH bump).
    2. Add a clearly formatted changelog section for version 1.4.3.
    3. Create a machine-readable audit trail of the bump.

    Required final state
    --------------------
    A. File: /home/user/project/VERSION  
       • Content must be exactly a single line containing only the new version string:
         1.4.3

    B. File: /home/user/project/CHANGELOG.md  
       • The file must keep its existing header and earlier release notes.  
       • A new release section must be inserted at the top, directly underneath the 
         introductory paragraph, with this exact block:

            ## [1.4.3] - 2023-08-26
            - Compliance: Added audit trail for version bump

       • The rest of the file must remain unmodified and immediately follow the new block.
         Make sure there is a single blank line between sections, exactly as shown in the
         example under “Expected final CHANGELOG.md” (see test harness).

    C. File: /home/user/audit/version_bump.log  
       • Create the directory /home/user/audit if it does not yet exist.  
       • Append (--do not overwrite) one and only one line in the exact CSV format below:

            2023-08-26T00:00:00Z,1.4.2->1.4.3,user

       • No leading or trailing whitespace.  
       • There should be **no additional lines** in this log file.

    Notes & constraints
    -------------------
    • Perform the update using any standard shell utilities available in a default Ubuntu
      environment; the whole task should require no more than 2-3 simple commands once you
      think it through.  
    • The agent does not have root privileges; all actions must occur inside /home/user.  
    • Do not hard-code the path using ~; always use absolute paths so the test suite can
      verify them.  
    • The automated grader will inspect the three files listed above; accuracy—including
      exact spacing and newlines—is required.  
    • You do NOT need to commit anything to git; only edit / create the files on disk.

    Deliverables
    ------------
    When finished, print a short confirmation message to the terminal so a human operator
    knows the task completed (content is free-form).  The test harness will rely exclusively
    on the file contents described above.
truth:
Initial state before the agent starts
    ------------------------------------
    1. /home/user/project exists with read/write permissions.
    2. /home/user/project/VERSION contains exactly:
           1.4.2\n
    3. /home/user/project/CHANGELOG.md contains exactly:

        # Changelog

        All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.

        ## [1.4.2] - 2023-08-20
        - Fixed security minor bug\n

    Expected state after successful completion
    ------------------------------------------
    1. /home/user/project/VERSION contains exactly:
           1.4.3\n
    2. /home/user/project/CHANGELOG.md contains exactly:

        # Changelog

        All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.

        ## [1.4.3] - 2023-08-26
        - Compliance: Added audit trail for version bump

        ## [1.4.2] - 2023-08-20
        - Fixed security minor bug\n

       (Note the two blank lines after the header paragraph, one blank line between sections,
        and a trailing newline at the end of file.)

    3. /home/user/audit/version_bump.log exists and contains exactly:
           2023-08-26T00:00:00Z,1.4.2->1.4.3,user\n
       and nothing else—only one line total.

    The grader will fail the task if any file deviates from the content or if additional
    whitespace/lines are present.
